methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Ideon Technologies is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Gary Agnew that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA), with direct peer benchmarking conducted against three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: KoBold Metals ($3.0B Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B Series C), and Amogy ($700M Series C).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage advancement, yielding $57M. Peer-median analysis across the three identified comps produces a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $3.0B. The median peer valuation substantially exceeds the stage-progression result, reflecting the capital intensity and long development timelines characteristic of climate tech.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $48M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $528M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in whether Ideon's technology trajectory and commercial traction align with higher-stage capital-intensive peers or warrant a valuation closer to typical Series B step-up economics. Key uncertainties include proof-of-concept progress, path to first revenue, and underlying technology readiness relative to the peer set.
